subject: [RFC PATCH 38/45] mm: page_alloc: per-(zone, order, mt) PASS_1 hint cache

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch introduces a hint cache to improve the performance of page allocation in Linux. The cache stores the most recently used superpage block (SPB) for each zone, order, and migration type tuple. This allows the kernel to quickly retrieve the next SPB to allocate from, reducing the number of list walks required. The patch also includes a mechanism to invalidate stale hints when the SPB array is resized due to memory hotplug additions. Measurements show a significant reduction in median walk depth and tail visits, with a hint hit rate of 99%. The memory cost of the cache is estimated to be around 320 bytes per zone plus 2.6 KB per CPU.
